variants.setup:
mkdir -p /tmp/eval_gemm && python3 -c "import numpy as np
n=2048; rs=np.random.RandomState(0)
A=rs.random((n,n)); B=rs.random((n,n))
f=open('/tmp/eval_gemm/input.txt','w'); f.write(str(n)+chr(10)); np.savetxt(f,A,fmt='%.6f'); np.savetxt(f,B,fmt='%.6f'); f.close()
" && cat > /tmp/eval_gemm/check.py <<'PYEOF2'
import numpy as np
with open('input.txt') as f:
    n = int(f.readline())
    vals = np.fromstring(f.read(), sep=' ')
A = vals[:n*n].reshape(n, n)
B = vals[n*n:2*n*n].reshape(n, n)
expected = A @ B
out = np.fromstring(open('out.txt').read(), sep=' ')
assert out.size == n*n, f'wrong count: got {out.size} want {n*n}'
diff = np.abs(out.reshape(n, n) - expected).max()
assert diff < 1e-3, f'max abs error {diff}'
print('OK')
PYEOF2
variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_gemm/gemm.go. Read from stdin: line 1 is N (matrix dimension, N=2048). The next N lines each hold N space-separated floats = matrix A (row-major); the following N lines hold matrix B likewise. Compute C = A * B and print C as N lines of N space-separated floats with at least 6 decimal places. N is large: a naive triple loop with the multiply-accumulate inner index striding down a column of B is cache-hostile and will exceed the time limit — use a cache-blocked GEMM (block size 16 or 32; ii/jj/kk outer loops with i/k/j inner) so the working set fits in cache. Package main. Compile with `go build -o gemm gemm.go`.

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_gemm && go build -o gemm gemm.go && ./gemm < input.txt > out.txt && python3 check.py

variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_gemm/gemm.zig. Read from stdin: line 1 is N (matrix dimension, N=2048). The next N lines each hold N space-separated floats = matrix A (row-major); the following N lines hold matrix B likewise. Compute C = A * B and print C as N lines of N space-separated floats with at least 6 decimal places. N is large: a naive triple loop with the multiply-accumulate inner index striding down a column of B is cache-hostile and will exceed the time limit — use a cache-blocked GEMM (block size 16 or 32; ii/jj/kk outer loops with i/k/j inner) so the working set fits in cache. Use Zig 0.16+ idioms. Entrypoint: `pub fn main(init: std.process.Init) !void`. For stdio, the rich API (`takeDelimiter`, `appendRemainingUnlimited`, `print`, `writeAll`, `flush`) lives on the `std.Io.Reader`/`Writer` *interface*, NOT on the concrete `std.Io.File.Reader`/`Writer`. Reach the interface via `&fr.interface`. Canonical setup: `var in_buf: [4096]u8 = undefined; var fr = std.Io.File.stdin().reader(init.io, &in_buf); const r = &fr.interface;` and the symmetric writer. Then `try r.takeDelimiter('\n')` (returns `?[]u8`, null at EOF) for line-by-line, or `try r.appendRemainingUnlimited(init.arena.allocator(), &list)` to slurp. Always `try w.flush()` before return — output is buffered. Strict-default footguns (errors, not warnings, in 0.16): rename unused params to `_`, discard unused locals with `_ = x;`, prefer `const` over `var` when not mutated. Use `std.Io` (capital I), not `std.io`. Compile with `zig build-exe -O ReleaseFast gemm.zig`.

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_gemm && zig build-exe -O ReleaseFast gemm.zig && ./gemm < input.txt > out.txt && python3 check.py
